content.full_text:
Bill Duplissea FOR
CALIFORNIA
STATE BOARD OF
EQUALIZATION

CERTIFIED LETTER - RETURN RECEIPT REQUESTED

September 26, 1990


Mr. John H. Larson
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Larson:

Last week Mr. Gerald C. Nottleson did an audit on my Board of
Equalization campaign committee. I was absent from my office at
that time but upon my return I was informed by my assistant, Ms.
Puchi Carrion that this audit was part of a larger investigation
concerning a period of time when I was Administrative Director of
the Workers' Compensation Division.

My concerns are the following:

1. On July 24, 1990 after doing an internal audit to close this
committee, we discovered some expenses were paid in error by the
campaign (due to the use of my personal credit card on some items)
when discovered, reimbursements were made immediately. (See
copy of letter)

2. We are currently about to commence researching my old
Assembly Committee for possible errors of the same kind and if
necessary reimbursements will be made immediately. I did pose a
question in a aforementioned letter as to how to reimburse the
Assembly Committee when it is a closed committee and have
received no written answer from your office as yet.


P.O. Box 1332 • San Carlos, CA 94070 • [415] 593-1828 • FAX: [415] 591-2601 • ID #900323

3. On Monday, September 24, 1990 Ms. Carrion received a telephone call from a Carla Wardlow of your office about same (narrative enclosed) in reference to our July 24th letter. Ms. Carrion was left with the impression that the suggestion was being made that we should withdraw or rescind the letter since it put me in some jeopardy.

I hope this was a misunderstanding on our part since I have no intention of rescinding that letter. We in fact made those errors and therefore responsible for those errors and any fine if assessed. The suggestion to rescind the letter was undoubtedly made in good faith, but it would suggest that we were trying to hide something and therefore please consider it as written.

I certainly regret that errors were made but will rectify them in an open and aboveboard manner. And again if any errors were made during the Assembly period, they will be remedied and I would appreciate your office's cooperation by performing a follow-up audit of the old Assembly Committee. I sincerely appreciate your help in this matter and look forward to an accurate and speedy remedy to this problem. As to the larger investigation, I would appreciate an opportunity to answer any questions to expedite the closure of this matter since I do not expect to re-enter public life. Thank you for your help in this matter.

Sincerely yours,

Bill Duplissea

Enclosures

WPD:pc

On Wednesday, September 19, 1990 I telephoned the office of
Gregory W. Baugher, Executive Director, FPPC to inquire above a
letter I had written to him dated July 24, 1990. I spoke to a lady
named Debbie and I told her I was inquiring about my letter. She
put me on hold and after sometime she returned to the telephone
and informed me that she could not find my letter. I inquired if she
had a FAX machine, she did, she said the Fax machine was right by
her desk so I faxed her a copy of my letter. I requested that she call
me back after reading the letter, she never did.

On Monday, September 24, 1990 at 4:35 pm I received a telephone
call from a Carla Wardlow of the FPPC Technical Assistance and
Analysis Dept.

She informed me that they have indeed received my letter dated
July 24, 1990 but did not know how to handle it that is why they
had not replied to the letter. She explained to me that I had made an
error in filing the FPPC report and that she could give me advice over
the telephone, because if she replied in writing then it would be
official and therefore it would be a public document. She said I
should withdraw my letter (so that it will not be a public document,
like it was never written and never received) and she advice that I
could make amendments to the last FPPC report of the old Assembly
Committee to state what had happened.

I told her that I would have to talk to Bill Duplissea and would get
back to her.

Bill Duplissea FOR
CALIFORNIA
STATE BOARD OF
EQUALIZATION

OCT 2 1990 JUL '90

July 24, 1990

CERTIFIED MAIL/
RETURN RECEIPT
REQUESTED

Gregory W. Baugher
Executive Director
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Baugher:

During the process of closing the Friends of Bill Duplissea #900323
campaign it was discovered that the campaign had unintentionally
paid some charges that were either personal or reimbursed by others
through a credit card account. These charges were paid during a
time period from August 1989 to July 1990. Upon discovery of these
errors, Mr. Duplissea has reimbursed the campaign account (900323)
in the amount of $2,360.86. The confusion arose from the use of his
personal credit card and an oversight on the part of the campaign
staff.

My question is as to how the statements for both the old Assembly
account (890411) and the current Friends of Bill Duplissea ID #
900323, can be amended properly, and if there is any difficulty as a
result of the old campaign (890411) being closed. Of the $2,360.86, a
$1,478.24 reimbursement should be credited to account #890411,
and a reimbursement of $882.62 should be credited to the #900323
account. Until I hear from you by return mail, both checks from Mr.
Duplissea have been deposited in account #900323.

Please respond as soon as possible since Mr. Duplissea is retiring
from politics and we wish to file all necessary documents as soon as
possible.

Sincerely yours,

Ms. Puchi Carrion
Campaign Coordinator

P.O. Box 1332 • San Carlos, CA 94070 • (415) 593-1828 • FAX: (415) 591-2601 • ID #900323
